creator: gallatin@cs.duke.edu
text:
FUJISHIMA Satsuki writes:
 > This port is not broken. You must update _entire_ port that is
 > required to build this port.
 > 
 > 1) cvsup ports-all
 > 2) cd /usr/ports/sysutils/portupgrade && make install clean
 > 3) portupgrade -CrN guile-gnome

The system is up-to-date as of July 30th.  (kernel/world/ports tree).
Has something major changed since then?

 > and you should be able to build guile-gnome.

Did you notice that this is an alpha?   Have you tried building it on
alpha?   

FWIW - Its obvious that nobody has tested gnome on alpha.
gnome-terminal as shipped with 4.3-RELEASE, as well as built from a
July 30th ports tree, will do its I/O in the parent, not in the
graphical terminal.  Eg:

<12:04am>thunder/gallatin:~>uname -srm
FreeBSD 4.3-STABLE alpha
<12:03am>thunder/gallatin:~>tty;echo $$
/dev/ttyp6
97527
<12:03am>thunder/gallatin:~>gnome-terminal
Warning: no access to tty (Inappropriate ioctl for device).
Thus no job control in this shell.
tcsh 6.10.00 (Astron) 2000-11-19 (alpha-digital-FreeBSD) options 8b,nls,dl,al,kan,sm,rh,color,dspm
setting up context sens completions....done
gallatin has logged on ttyp0 from :0.0
gallatin has logged on ttyp7 from :0.0
gallatin has logged on ttyv0 from local
<12:03am>thunder/gallatin:~>tty;echo $$
/dev/ttyp6
97547
<12:04am>thunder/gallatin:~>exit
<12:04am>thunder/gallatin:~>exit
<12:04am>thunder/gallatin:~>

I was going to look into it a little more before opening a PR on it..
Ideas would be welcome.

Drew
